summary.intent: Write a comprehensive README for the cc repository and get it merged via a pull request, including handling automated review feedback.
summary.outcome: Draft PR #92 was created and three automated review comments (from gemini-code-assist and copilot) were addressed by fixing the README: adding a catch-up usage example, clarifying Bun as a prerequisite, and expanding abbreviated tool subcommands to their full forms.

repo: Repository uses a docs-readme branch for README updates (file at docs-readme/README.md)
Automated reviewers active on PRs: gemini-code-assist and GitHub Copilot
Repository labels include 'documentation' for docs-only PRs
Repo is owned by ronnnnn on GitHub (ronnnnn/cc)

code.finding: catch-up command usage example was missing and needed to be added
Bun was not listed as a prerequisite, which was flagged as an issue for developers not using asdf/mise
Abbreviated subcommands (e.g., 'asdf i', 'mise i') were replaced with full-form commands for clarity and correctness

workflow: asdf is not installed in the local environment, making direct CLI verification of asdf subcommands impossible — fell back to documentation-based reasoning
mise i is a valid abbreviation for mise install, but full-form commands are preferred in documentation
A background monitor script was written to /tmp/pr-monitor-92.sh to watch for PR events and trigger fixes automatically
PR was created as Draft to allow iteration before requesting full review

friction: asdf not installed locally, preventing direct verification of whether 'asdf i' is a valid subcommand — had to make a judgment call based on docs
Copilot flagged 'asdf i' as non-standard but this could not be confirmed or denied from the local environment
open_items: PR #92 is still in Draft state — needs to be marked ready for review and merged
Review comments were addressed but threads may not have been formally resolved/replied to on GitHub
Re-review from gemini-code-assist and copilot after the fixes has not yet occurred
